agent_output: {"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Bird defines courier assignment before IN_TRANSIT as a domain invariant (must always be true). MJ's architecture explicitly violates this invariant by allowing IN_TRANSIT without a courier, deferring assignment to a background job within 30 seconds. These are mutually exclusive positions on a core state transition rule.","agents_involved":["Bird","MJ"],"options":["Option A: Enforce Bird's invariant strictly — require courier assignment before IN_TRANSIT. Customer acknowledgement speed may decrease, but domain integrity is preserved. The state machine blocks the transition until assignment completes.","Option B: Relax the invariant with Bird's explicit approval — introduce an intermediate state (e.g., AWAITING_COURIER or TRANSIT_PENDING) that acknowledges the order without claiming it is in transit. This preserves the semantic truth of IN_TRANSIT (courier is assigned) while achieving MJ's goal of fast customer acknowledgement.","Option C: Accept MJ's architecture as-is — Bird formally downgrades the rule from invariant to eventual-consistency guarantee (courier assigned within N seconds of IN_TRANSIT). This changes the domain model's correctness contract and Bird must explicitly sign off."],"recommendation":"Option B is the strongest compromise. It satisfies Bird's invariant (IN_TRANSIT always has a courier) while achieving MJ's latency goal (fast customer response via the intermediate state). However, Bird must decide whether the domain actually has this intermediate concept or whether it is an artificial technical seam. Bird and MJ need to align before any handoff to Shaq."}],"confidence":{"level":35,"high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction is real and clearly identified — these two outputs cannot both be implemented as stated"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which resolution option is correct — this requires Bird (domain authority) and MJ (architect) to negotiate","Whether an intermediate state exists in the real domain or would be a technical invention"],"assumptions":["Bird's use of 'invariant: true' means this rule must hold at all times, not eventually","MJ's 30-second window is a hard requirement from a product/UX perspective, not just a nice-to-have"]}}
